spec_match.rationale: The live SVG now renders through the inline textPath path attribute, exposes startOffset, method, and spacing, and shows href fallback behavior.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised text entry, sliders, method and spacing selects, shape buttons, fallback buttons, and pause/play with live DOM and code updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede and live code now match the page behavior: animated text uses the inline path attribute rather than only showing it in a snippet.

edge_cases.rationale: The page includes remove-path and empty-path modes that expose the href fallback and rendered text box readout.

references.rationale: The page includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5157546589552640 reference link and the SVG 2 textPath path attribute link.

design_system.rationale: The repaired page uses shared CSS variables, pairs local button backgrounds with colors, avoids inline handlers and inline styles, and Lighthouse reports Accessibility 100.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome 148 through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v147/support-path-attribute-on-svg-textpath-element/animated-text-path/?verify=final-svg-textpath-repair. Exercised text input, font-size, duration, startOffset, method, spacing, shape buttons, remove path, empty path, restore path, and pause/play; the textPath retained a real path attribute, rendered text, and surfaced fallback state in the live readout. The feature conformance route reported 9 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console messages or failed requests.
